title: Writing - Part 2
description: Choose one of the following writing tasks and write your answer in 140-190 words.

tasks.title: Report
tasks.prompt:
Your school technology committee has asked you to write a report on students' use of smartphones and tablets during the school day. Include information about how students use their devices, the impact this has on learning, and recommendations for a new school device policy.

Write your report.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Report on Student Device Use During the School Day

Introduction
This report presents findings from a survey of 200 students regarding their use of smartphones and tablets during lessons, with recommendations for a revised school policy.

Current Device Use
The survey revealed that 85% of students bring smartphones to school daily. While some students use devices productively — for example, to look up information or access digital textbooks — a significant majority (62%) admitted to checking social media or messaging friends during lessons.

Impact on Learning
Teachers reported that device use has led to reduced attention spans and increased difficulty maintaining classroom focus. However, several teachers also noted that tablets, when used appropriately, enhance engagement and support independent research.

Recommendations
The committee is advised to implement a balanced approach: phones should be stored in lockers during lessons, while school-provided tablets remain available for educational purposes. A clear acceptable use policy should be communicated to all students and parents. Regular digital literacy sessions would also help students use technology responsibly.

tasks.title: Article
tasks.prompt:
You see this notice in an English-language magazine:

'Technology is changing our lives faster than ever before. Write an article about a piece of technology that has changed your life and discuss whether you think modern gadgets make us more or less connected to the world around us.'

Write your article.
tasks.modelAnswer:
My Smartphone: Window to the World or Wall Between Us?

When I got my first smartphone at the age of thirteen, it felt like being handed the keys to the universe. Suddenly I could access any piece of information, connect with friends across the globe, and navigate any city without getting lost. A decade later, I remain both amazed and slightly unnerved by this small device.

There is no question that smartphones have made us more connected in certain ways. Video calls mean I can see my grandparents, who live hundreds of kilometres away, every single week. News from across the world reaches me instantly. I can learn new skills, discover new music, and explore new ideas — all from the palm of my hand.

And yet, I sometimes wonder whether we are losing something in the process. When everyone at a dinner table is staring at their phone, are we truly connected? When we document every moment for social media, do we actually experience it?

Technology is a tool. How we choose to use it is entirely up to us.

tasks.title: Letter
tasks.prompt:
You have read an advertisement for a new gadget that promises to improve productivity and reduce stress. However, you tried the product and were very disappointed. Write a letter of complaint to the company explaining your experience and requesting a refund.

Write your letter.
tasks.modelAnswer:
Dear Customer Services,

I am writing to express my dissatisfaction with the FocusFlow Smart Planner I purchased from your website three weeks ago (Order Reference: FF-48291). I was drawn to the product by its promises of improved productivity and reduced stress, but I am sorry to say that my experience has been deeply disappointing.

Upon receiving the device, I discovered that the battery life is considerably shorter than advertised — lasting approximately four hours rather than the claimed twelve. Furthermore, the accompanying app is poorly designed and frequently crashes, making it impossible to use the gadget's core features.

Despite following all troubleshooting steps outlined in the manual and contacting your technical support team twice, the issues have not been resolved. I no longer have confidence that the product will perform as advertised.

I would therefore like to request a full refund of the purchase price of £89.99. I am prepared to return the item in its original packaging at your convenience.

I look forward to your prompt response.

Yours sincerely,
Peter Nguyen
